description: Create the complete AST-rewrite-repair Harbor Terminal-Bench task from scratch, including all source modules with embedded bugs, config files, data files, tests, repair script, Dockerfile, instruction.md, and task.toml.
steps: Step 1: Create environment/Dockerfile with python:3.11-slim base, pip install uv, COPY runtime/ /app/runtime/, WORKDIR /app
Step 2: Create environment/runtime/__init__.py (empty)
Step 3: Create environment/runtime/config/__init__.py (empty)

Step 4: Create environment/runtime/config/transforms.ini with configparser format containing: [project] section with project_id and active_rules (with Bug A: space before dead_code in comma-separated list), [scoping] section with max_depth=10, [scoping.strict] section with max_depth=3 (Bug B: code reads from wrong section), [rules] section with rule definitions and priorities, [output] section
Step 5: Create environment/runtime/hasher.py - deterministic SHA-256[:16] hashing for transform_report and rewrite_manifest (same pattern as existing hasher.py)

Step 6: Create environment/runtime/rule_loader.py with Bug A embedded - loads active_rules from config using .split(',') WITHOUT .strip() on items. Has a rule registry dict mapping rule names to handler info. The space before 'dead_code' means ' dead_code' never matches the registry key 'dead_code'

Step 7: Create environment/runtime/scope_resolver.py with Bug B + Bug E embedded - reads max_depth from config section [scoping] instead of [scoping.strict] (gets 10 instead of 3), AND caches this value at module import time with _cached_depth = _load_scope_depth() pattern. Has a comment referencing 'uses scoping.strict for bounded traversal' in a docstring or nearby but the actual code reads wrong section

Step 8: Create environment/runtime/visitor.py with Bug C embedded - AST visitor that processes .src files, maintains a symbol_table dict for variable bindings. The symbol_table is initialized once and accumulates across file passes instead of being reset per-file. Has a red herring flush_cache() method that clears a DIFFERENT cache (the _parse_cache for AST nodes, not the symbol_table). Processing logic: parses DECL/FUNC/CALL/IF/NESTED/ASSIGN statements, builds symbol table, determines which nodes are candidates for inlining/renaming/dead-code-elimination

Step 9: Create environment/runtime/rule_chain.py with Bug D embedded - applies transformation rules to AST nodes. When multiple rules match same line, sorts by (line_no, rule_priority) instead of correct (line_no, rule_name, rule_priority). Has comment '# Note: priority is assigned per rule category, not globally unique'. Also has a comment mentioning 'uses scoping.strict for bounded traversal' to hint at Bug B

Step 10: Create environment/runtime/run_rewriter.py - main entry point that orchestrates: loads config, loads source files from data/, calls visitor to process each file, applies rule chain, generates transform_report.json and rewrite_manifest.json. Imports all modules, produces structurally valid output even with bugs

Step 11: Create environment/runtime/data/source_alpha.src - ~25 lines of custom mini-language with DECL, FUNC, CALL, NESTED statements. Include some dead variables that dead_code rule should eliminate. Include nested blocks at various depths

Step 12: Create environment/runtime/data/source_beta.src - ~22 lines with different variable names, some duplicates of names from alpha (to trigger Bug C cross-file pollution), NESTED blocks going deeper than depth 3
Step 13: Create environment/runtime/data/source_gamma.src - ~20 lines with its own symbols, should have specific expected transform count when processed independently (for test 7)
Step 14: Create environment/runtime/output/.gitkeep (empty file for git tracking of output dir)
Step 15: Create tests/test.sh - runs python3 -m runtime.run_rewriter then uses 'uv run --with pytest pytest /tests/test_rewriter.py -v', writes reward to /logs/verifier/reward.txt

Step 16: Create tests/test_rewriter.py with 10 graduated tests: 4 easy (always pass - structural checks), 3 medium (need 2-3 fixes - check active_rules==4, scope_depth==3, gamma transforms count), 3 hard (need 4-5 fixes - operation ordering, integrity hash, combined consistency). Use vague error messages for medium. Include ordering hint (line, rule_name, priority) in hard test
Step 17: Create solution/solve.sh - runs 'python3 /solution/repair_rewriter.py' from /app

Step 18: Create solution/repair_rewriter.py - applies 5 fixes via string replacement: (A) add .strip() to rule list parsing, (B) change config section from scoping to scoping.strict, (C) reset symbol_table between files, (D) add rule_name to sort key, (E) invalidate cached scope depth. Then re-runs the system
Step 19: Create task.toml with the specified metadata

Step 20: Create instruction.md describing the system abstractly with 'Global system-wide tooling' phrase, mentioning 'each file processed independently with isolated symbol scope', mentioning 'transformation ordering uses (line, rule_name, priority) composite key', using absolute paths, no word 'pipeline'
Step 21: Verify NOP (buggy) run produces valid output (4 easy tests pass, 6 fail) by running the system locally and checking
Step 22: Verify Oracle (repair script) produces correct output (all 10 tests pass) by applying fixes and re-running
acceptance_criteria: System produces structurally valid transform_report.json and rewrite_manifest.json with buggy code (never crashes)
4 easy tests pass with buggy code (structural validation only)
6 medium+hard tests fail with buggy code
All 10 tests pass after repair script runs
Bug E (cached scope depth) is invisible until Bug B is fixed
No BUG/FIXME/TODO/HACK comments anywhere in source
All paths in instruction.md are absolute (/app/runtime/...)
verification: Run python3 -m runtime.run_rewriter from /app - system does not crash
Run tests with buggy code - exactly 4 pass (easy tests)
Run repair script then re-run tests - all 10 pass
grep for BUG/FIXME/TODO/HACK in source files - none found
